jokes.text: I just came back from the free clinic, and guess what I got? The phone number of those two nurses we met.
jokes.setup: Coming back from free clinic suggests getting medical results
jokes.punchline: He got phone numbers instead of test results
jokes.explanation: Solid misdirection with good economy - sets up medical concern then subverts with romantic pursuit

jokes.text: Yo, the twins that sleep in the same bed?
jokes.punchline: Implying they share a bed together
jokes.explanation: Basic sexual innuendo, functional but not particularly clever

jokes.text: Actually, cover my next three shifts. You know how demanding twins can be.
jokes.punchline: Escalating the sexual implication with 'demanding'
jokes.explanation: Builds on previous joke with escalation, competent follow-through

jokes.text: Oh, wait. I just remembered, I can't go. Well, why not? I'm getting married.
jokes.setup: Jazz seems to remember a conflict
jokes.punchline: The conflict is getting married - completely unexpected
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection with perfect economy - completely shifts the episode's direction with casual delivery

jokes.text: I saw her on Cops, getting busted for grand theft auto.
jokes.punchline: He met her by watching her get arrested on TV
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd concept that's perfectly in character for Jazz - original and memorable

jokes.text: Jazz, the woman's a convict? We prefer the term 'freedom impaired.'
jokes.punchline: Jazz's euphemistic correction
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that parodies politically correct language - highly quotable

jokes.text: How about the term 'not playing with a full cell block'?
jokes.punchline: Will's prison-themed twist on 'not playing with a full deck'
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that transforms a common idiom with perfect thematic relevance - very quotable

jokes.text: But the truth is, behind these shades, there's a big, empty hole.
jokes.setup: Jazz trying to be deep and philosophical
jokes.punchline: The melodramatic revelation delivered while wearing sunglasses indoors
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jazz's pretentious side, enhanced by his signature sunglasses

jokes.text: Well, that's for damn sure.
jokes.punchline: Will's blunt agreement
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective reaction - Will's matter-of-fact agreement undercuts Jazz's attempt at depth

jokes.text: My other friends pretend to like me for my money. That's because you owe it to them.
jokes.setup: Jazz says friends pretend to like him for money
jokes.punchline: Will reveals it's because Jazz owes them money
jokes.explanation: Nice subversion of Jazz's self-pity with a logical explanation that reveals his character flaws

jokes.text: Why not. It's free.
jokes.setup: Will questioning getting married at prison
jokes.punchline: Jazz's practical reasoning
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment - Jazz's cheapness overrides any romantic considerations

jokes.text: We follow an execution.
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of following an execution
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny escalation delivered with Jazz's characteristic obliviousness to inappropriateness

jokes.text: Officer down. Officer down. We need backup. Get her off of me. She's biting my...
jokes.punchline: Increasingly violent and absurd arrest details
jokes.explanation: Standard escalation gag that establishes Jewel's violent tendencies for later payoffs.

jokes.text: What's wrong with her face? Sweetheart, it's electronically scrambled.
jokes.setup: Looking at scrambled police footage of Jewel
jokes.punchline: Hilary doesn't understand video scrambling
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showcasing Hilary's airheaded nature with technical concepts

jokes.text: Up till today, Jazz was available. Now that he's getting married... well, forbidden fruit always looks more tempting, doesn't it?
jokes.punchline: She's attracted to him now that he's unavailable
jokes.explanation: Clever psychological observation that creates an unexpected character moment for Vivian

jokes.text: Mother, for God's sake, he's half your age.
jokes.punchline: Hilary's scandalized reaction
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction beat, functional but not particularly inventive

jokes.text: I give it three weeks. The marriage or her parole?
jokes.punchline: Carlton's clarification about what might end first
jokes.explanation: Excellent structure with perfect economy - dark humor that gets multiple laughs from one setup

jokes.text: But, you know... But it wasn't like I was gonna need them. After all, I wasn't gonna have any fun anymore.
jokes.setup: Phil talking about losing friends when married
jokes.punchline: His resigned acceptance that marriage means no fun
jokes.explanation: Relatable married man humor, competent but familiar territory

jokes.text: Man, Uncle Phil, I knew you had a man-size appetite, but both feet?
jokes.setup: Phil presumably has both feet in his mouth from his marriage comments
jokes.punchline: Will comments on his metaphorical foot-eating with food reference
jokes.explanation: Clever connection between Phil's size and the 'foot in mouth' idiom

jokes.text: I mean, even though I am just... a poor black man trying to get my education.
jokes.setup: Will trying to guilt Phil into paying
jokes.punchline: Over-the-top sob story manipulation
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's manipulation skills with theatrical delivery

jokes.text: If Jazz would just find some bimbo to get his mind off of Hilary... I'd be more than happy to pay for the wedding.
jokes.setup: Will reminding Phil of previous statement
jokes.punchline: Phil's past promise coming back to haunt him
jokes.explanation: Solid callback that creates accountability for Phil's past statements

jokes.text: I could call it a rehabilitation party and write it off.
jokes.punchline: Finding a tax loophole for the wedding
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Phil's lawyer mindset finding clever justification

jokes.text: Check it out, you're a judge. You can perform the ceremony. Pay yourself 25 bucks. Fifty. Pay yourself 100. It's your money.
jokes.punchline: The escalating self-payment amounts
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing Phil getting caught up in the financial logic

jokes.text: Marry me, Carlton.
jokes.setup: Will presumably playing with a bride doll
jokes.punchline: Making the doll propose to Carlton
jokes.explanation: Simple physical gag with good economy but relies on basic doll play humor

jokes.text: Very funny, Will. But while you're playing with dolls, I'll be securing the premises. I mean, we are gonna have a convict in the house.
jokes.setup: Carlton responding to Will's doll play
jokes.punchline: His paranoid overreaction to having a convict as guest
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showcasing Carlton's uptight, paranoid nature

jokes.text: Oh, is she pregnant too?
jokes.setup: Jazz saying Jewel is 'due any minute'
jokes.punchline: Phil misunderstanding 'due' as pregnancy vs arrival
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection based on wordplay with good economy

jokes.text: Somebody wants to know where the bride is registered. Gillespie Liquors.
jokes.punchline: Jazz's fiancée is registered at a liquor store
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of wedding tradition that fits the convict bride character

jokes.text: I also put my diamond cuff links in a balloon and swallowed them.
jokes.punchline: Extreme measure of hiding valuables internally
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd escalation that perfectly captures Carlton's paranoid overpreparation

jokes.text: That's more than I ever wanted to know about you, Carlton.
jokes.punchline: Will's disgusted reaction
jokes.explanation: Solid reaction that acknowledges the absurdity without being too clever

jokes.text: Hilary, will you get out of my way? You're blocking my view.
jokes.setup: Jewel arrives and presumably everyone wants to see her
jokes.punchline: Hilary is literally blocking Will's view of the convict bride
jokes.explanation: Basic visual gag setup, functional but not particularly creative

jokes.text: There's a lot to be said for a woman who's been in prison a long time.
jokes.punchline: Phil's inappropriate appreciation for sexually starved women
jokes.explanation: Dark humor that's somewhat out of character for Phil but gets a reaction

jokes.text: Hey. Yo. Hello in there.
jokes.setup: Jazz and Jewel presumably still kissing
jokes.punchline: Will trying to get their attention during extended kiss
jokes.explanation: Basic reaction to awkward public display, functional timing

jokes.text: Oh, yeah? As far as I can see, this here's the best man. I love this bony butt.
jokes.setup: Will introducing himself as best man
jokes.punchline: Jewel immediately hitting on Will, calling him the 'best man' literally
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay on 'best man' with immediate establishment of Jewel's forward nature

jokes.text: I also noticed you have a very small head.
jokes.punchline: Jewel's blunt, inappropriate observation
jokes.explanation: Perfect character establishment - Jewel's prison-honed bluntness deflating Carlton's pretension

jokes.text: Thanks, and I guess it's true what they say: Can't buy good taste. Hey.
jokes.setup: Vivian welcoming Jewel to their home
jokes.punchline: Convict insulting their taste despite being in mansion
jokes.explanation: Good irony with Jewel criticizing the wealthy family's taste while being their guest

jokes.text: See, the prison therapist says that I should say what's on my mind... and not keep too much bottled up, otherwise I'd... you know, go out and do something stupid like hotwiring a Lexus.
jokes.punchline: Therapy advice to prevent her from stealing cars again
jokes.explanation: Clever justification that ties her criminal past to her current behavior through therapy logic

jokes.text: Although it did get me on prime time.
jokes.punchline: Positive spin on being arrested on TV
jokes.explanation: Great callback with perfect deadpan delivery - finding the silver lining in criminal activity

jokes.text: I just wanna say, if you ever hurt him... I'll hunt you down like the dog that you are, and kill you.
jokes.punchline: Escalates to graphic death threat
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from sweet to terrifying that perfectly establishes her dangerous nature

jokes.text: Well, gotta go shop for your present.
jokes.punchline: Casually transitions to wedding gift shopping
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic timing - the casual shift from threat to mundane activity

jokes.text: Beautiful and nuts. You must be very proud.
jokes.punchline: Will's assessment to Jazz
jokes.explanation: Succinct summary that captures the situation with good economy

jokes.text: You know, a cup of coffee, a blood test.
jokes.setup: Will offering to help Jewel with anything she needs
jokes.punchline: Suggesting she needs an STD test
jokes.explanation: Good escalation from mundane to pointed, though somewhat obvious given her background

jokes.text: It's already zipped up. I know. I want you to zip it down.
jokes.setup: Will noting dress is already zipped
jokes.punchline: She wants him to unzip it
jokes.explanation: Clean misdirection that sets up the seduction scenario

jokes.text: You know... if somebody was to walk in here right now... they might think you was hitting on me.
jokes.punchline: She pretends it would be a misunderstanding
jokes.explanation: Good ironic setup where she's clearly the aggressor but frames it as his fault

jokes.text: Trust me, if I was hitting on you, you'd know it.
jokes.punchline: Will's confident assertion of his seduction skills
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's cockiness even in awkward situation

jokes.text: No, no, no. Uncle Phil, this was like one of them Fatal Attraction elevator-scene kisses.
jokes.setup: Phil thinks Will is exaggerating about the kiss
jokes.punchline: Specific movie reference to convey intensity
jokes.explanation: Effective pop culture reference that conveys the dangerous sexual energy

jokes.text: She was like a little naked Velcro midget.
jokes.setup: Will describing how Jewel stuck to him
jokes.punchline: Absurd metaphor combining multiple unexpected elements
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd visual metaphor that's both specific and hilarious - highly quotable

jokes.text: Next thing I know, she leans across... and fastens my seat belt. You know what I'm saying?
jokes.setup: Phil building up to something sexual
jokes.punchline: Innocent seat belt fastening delivered with innuendo
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Phil's innocent nature even when trying to be worldly

jokes.text: Please don't do that, Uncle Phil.
jokes.punchline: Will's uncomfortable reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat that acknowledges Phil's awkwardness

jokes.text: Oh, yeah. With a bar stool, on the head.
jokes.setup: Will asking if friend thanked Phil for honesty
jokes.punchline: Friend attacked him with bar stool
jokes.explanation: Good dark punchline with specific detail that makes the violence funnier

jokes.text: I gotta find somebody else to talk to about this.
jokes.setup: Phil's advice about telling the truth
jokes.punchline: Will immediately rejects the wisdom
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Will's avoidance of difficult solutions

jokes.text: She touched me, Will. And I know she'll touch you too.
jokes.punchline: Innocent meaning with unintended sexual subtext
jokes.explanation: Good dramatic irony since audience knows Jewel literally touched Will

jokes.text: We're gonna have 10 kids. One for every month of the year.
jokes.punchline: His bad math (12 months, wants 10 kids)
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Jazz's mathematical deficiency in romantic context

jokes.text: But we gonna name them all after you. Jazz, hey, you ain't got to do that, man. Okay, then, we'll name them Earl.
jokes.setup: Jazz wanting to name kids after Will
jokes.punchline: Random switch to naming them all Earl
jokes.explanation: Absurd compromise that shows Jazz's simple logic and random naming choice

jokes.text: We gonna keep our kids out of reform school.
jokes.setup: Jazz talking about their parenting goals
jokes.punchline: Their aspiration is just avoiding juvenile detention
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny low bar for parenting success that reflects their backgrounds

jokes.text: Oh, I just wanted to thank you... for picking out these lovely yellow-and-green ensembles.
jokes.setup: Will about to confess about Jewel
jokes.punchline: Chickens out and complains about ugly wedding outfits instead
jokes.explanation: Shows Will's cowardice through deflection to trivial complaint

jokes.text: And now, if I can just have your thumb.
jokes.setup: Carlton having guests sign guest book
jokes.punchline: Also wanting fingerprints like police booking
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation of Carlton's paranoid security measures

jokes.text: You know, I resent being treated like a common thief. Oh, you do, do you? Well, what kind of cold-hearted, vicious, evil crime did you commit? Securities fraud.
jokes.punchline: Reveals white-collar crime after Carlton's dramatic buildup
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of expectation - Carlton expects violent crime, gets financial crime instead.

jokes.text: You know, I resent being treated like a common thief. Oh, you do, do you? Well, what kind of cold-hearted, vicious, evil crime did you commit? Securities fraud.
jokes.punchline: Reveals white-collar crime after Carlton's dramatic buildup
jokes.explanation: Great misdirection contrasting Carlton's violent expectations with corporate crime

jokes.text: Really? An inside trader? So are you here alone?
jokes.punchline: Phil immediately interested in dating her
jokes.explanation: Shows Phil's priorities - more interested in financial crimes than violent ones

jokes.text: Are you kidding? Jazz ain't got no friends. I hired these people.
jokes.punchline: Will revealing he hired fake wedding guests
jokes.explanation: Excellent revelation that recontextualizes the whole scene and shows Will's dedication

jokes.text: Oh, my God. What am I saying?
jokes.punchline: Immediate horror at her own words
jokes.explanation: Standard reaction showing character's immediate regret

jokes.text: I ain't never owned anything that still had the serial number on it.
jokes.punchline: Implies everything he owns is stolen
jokes.explanation: Perfect character revelation showing Jazz's criminal lifestyle through small detail

jokes.text: No, really, I can't, I'm on parole, I'm not allowed to leave the state.
jokes.setup: Will telling them to leave state
jokes.punchline: Practical legal restriction prevents romantic escape
jokes.explanation: Good reality check that grounds the drama in Jewel's actual legal situation

jokes.text: Damn these good looks of mine.
jokes.setup: Jewel can't leave because of Will's appeal
jokes.punchline: Will blaming his attractiveness for the situation
jokes.explanation: Classic Will vanity moment that's perfectly in character

jokes.text: And on top of that, you ain't even that good-looking.
jokes.punchline: Hilary's final insult about his looks
jokes.explanation: Good character moment with Hilary delivering final blow to Will's ego

jokes.text: Well, damn.
jokes.punchline: Will's deflated reaction
jokes.explanation: Simple reaction beat with good timing but nothing special

jokes.text: You know, whenever we go to a nice restaurant, he carries my tray.
jokes.punchline: Their 'nice restaurant' is apparently fast food
jokes.explanation: Great class contrast showing their different definition of 'nice restaurant'

jokes.text: And when it's time to skip out on the check... he always holds open the bathroom window for me.
jokes.punchline: The 'romantic' gesture is helping her commit theft
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation that reframes criminal activity as romantic courtesy

jokes.text: Do you know where my mama was married? No, where? I don't know. I was hoping you did.
jokes.setup: Jewel asking about her mother's wedding location
jokes.punchline: She doesn't know and was hoping Will somehow would
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection that reveals character's tragic family situation through absurd logic

jokes.text: You're one jewel I never wanna hock.
jokes.punchline: Pun on Jewel's name and pawning jewelry
jokes.explanation: Good pun that's perfectly in character for Jazz's cheap romantic gestures

jokes.text: Sometimes I wanna bite you to see if you're really real.
jokes.punchline: Bizarre physical verification method for reality
jokes.explanation: Perfectly bizarre Jazz logic that's somehow endearing in its weird sincerity.

jokes.text: If love was craps, you'd be a seven. If it was on the first roll. But if it was on the second roll, and the point was six, you'd be a six. No, actually, you'd be a nine. I'd play the field on the second roll, make a couple of extra bucks. Then you'd be a six on the third roll, and then I'd clean up.
jokes.setup: Jazz trying to make romantic gambling metaphor
jokes.punchline: Gets completely lost in actual gambling strategy
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment showing Jazz losing romantic thread in gambling details

jokes.text: You know what I'm trying to say. I love you, and you make me feel lucky.
jokes.punchline: Brings it back to simple romantic point
jokes.explanation: Good recovery that shows Jazz's genuine feelings beneath the confusion

jokes.text: Thank you for setting this little jailbird free... body, mind and spirit.
jokes.punchline: Prison metaphor for emotional liberation
jokes.explanation: Sweet prison metaphor that fits Jewel's background while expressing genuine sentiment.
